channel.title: Transformer Circuits (Unofficial)
channel.description: Unofficial RSS feed generated from the homepage of transformer-circuits.pub

item.title: Verbalizable Representations Form a Global Workspace in Language Models Gurnee et al., 2026
item.description: We find that Claude maintains a small, privileged set of representations it can report on, control, and reason with, atop a much larger volume of automatic processing.

item.title: Natural Language Autoencoders Produce Unsupervised Explanations of LLM Activations Fraser-Taliente, Kantamneni, Ong, et al., 2026
item.description: We train Claude to translate its internal state into natural language.

item.title: Circuits Updates — May 2026
item.description: A short update on understanding features through downstream connections.

item.title: Circuits Updates — June 2026
item.description: A short update on turn-averaged sparse autoencoders.

item.title: HeadVis
item.description: We develop an interactive visualization tool to help us understand the behaviors of attention heads in language models.

item.title: Emotion Concepts and their Function in a Large Language Model Sofroniew et al., 2026
item.description: We find representations of emotion concepts in Claude Sonnet 4.5 and show that they causally influence its outputs.

item.title: Circuits Updates — September 2025
item.description: A small update on features and in-context learning.

item.title: Circuits Updates — October 2025
item.description: Small updates on visual features and dictionary initialization.

item.title: Circuits Updates — November 2025
item.description: A short update on harm pressure.

item.title: When Models Manipulate Manifolds: The Geometry of a Counting Task Gurnee et al., 2025
item.description: We find geometric structure underlying the mechanisms of a fundamental language model behavior.

item.title: Circuits Updates — July 2025
item.description: A collection of small updates: revisiting A Mathematical Framework and applications of interpretability to biology.

item.title: Circuits Updates — January 2025
item.description: A collection of small updates: dictionary learning optimization techniques.

item.title: Emergent Introspective Awareness in Large Language Models Lindsey, 2025
item.description: We find evidence that language models can introspect on their internal states.

item.title: A Toy Model of Interference Weights
item.description: Unpacking "interference weights" in some more depth.

item.title: A Toy Model of Mechanistic (Un)Faithfulness
item.description: When transcoders go awry.

item.title: Insights on Crosscoder Model Diffing
item.description: A preliminary note on using crosscoders to diff models.

item.title: Sparse mixtures of linear transforms
item.description: We investigate sparse mixture of linear transforms (MOLT), a new approach to transcoders.

item.title: Circuits Updates — August 2025
item.description: A small update: How does a persona modify the assistant’s response?

item.title: Circuit Tracing: Revealing Computational Graphs in Language Models Ameisen et al., 2025
item.description: We describe an approach to tracing the "step-by-step" computation involved when a model responds to a single prompt.

item.title: On the Biology of a Large Language Model Lindsey et al., 2025
item.description: We investigate the internal mechanisms used by Claude 3.5 Haiku — Anthropic's lightweight production model — in a variety of contexts.

item.title: Progress on Attention
item.description: An update on our progress studying attention.

item.title: Tracing Attention Computation Through Feature Interactions Kamath et al., 2025
item.description: We describe and apply a method to explain attention patterns in terms of feature interactions, and integrate this information into attribution graphs.

item.title: Circuits Updates — April 2025
item.description: A collection of small updates: jailbreaks, dense features, and spinning up on interpretability.

item.title: Circuits Updates — September 2024
item.description: A collection of small updates: investigating successor heads, oversampling data in SAEs.

item.title: Scaling Monosemanticity: Extracting Interpretable Features from Claude 3 Sonnet Templeton et al., 2024
item.description: Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a large number of interpretable features from Claude 3 Sonnet. Some appear to be safety-relevant.

item.title: Reflections on Qualitative Research
item.description: Some opinionated thoughts on why interpretability research may have qualitative aspects be more central than we're used to in other fields.

item.title: Stage-Wise Model Diffing
item.description: A preliminary note on model diffing through dictionary fine-tuning.

item.title: Circuits Updates — March 2024
item.description: A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.

item.title: Circuits Updates — June 2024
item.description: A collection of small updates: topk and gated SAE investigation.

item.title: Circuits Updates — July 2024
item.description: A collection of small updates: five hurdles, linear representations, dark matter, pivot tables, feature sensitivity.

item.title: Circuits Updates — January 2024
item.description: A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.

item.title: Circuits Updates — February 2024
item.description: A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.

item.title: Using Dictionary Learning Features as Classifiers
item.description: A preliminary note comparing feature-based and raw-activation based harmfulness classifiers.

item.title: Sparse Crosscoders for Cross-Layer Features and Model Diffing
item.description: A preliminary note on a way to get consistent features across layers, and even models.

item.title: Circuits Updates — August 2024
item.description: A collection of small updates: interpretability evals, reproducing self-explanation.

item.title: Circuits Updates — April 2024
item.description: A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.

item.title: Superposition, Memorization, and Double Descent Henighan et al., 2023
item.description: We have little mechanistic understanding of how deep learning models overfit to their training data, despite it being a central problem. Here we extend our previous work on toy models to shed light on how models generalize beyond their training data.

item.title: Distributed Representations: Composition & Superposition
item.description: An informal note on how "distributed representations" might be understood as two different, competing strategies — "composition" and "superposition" — with quite different properties.

item.title: Privileged Bases in the Transformer Residual Stream
item.description: Our mathematical theories of the Transformer architecture suggest that individual coordinates in the residual stream should have no special significance, but recent work has shown that this observation is false in practice. We investigate this phenomenon and provisionally conclude that the per-dimension normalizers in the Adam optimizer are to blame for the effect.

item.title: Towards Monosemanticity: Decomposing Language Models With Dictionary Learning Bricken et al., 2023
item.description: Using a sparse autoencoder, we extract a large number of interpretable features from a one-layer transformer.

item.title: Circuits Updates — May 2023
item.description: A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.

item.title: Circuits Updates — July 2023
item.description: A collection of small updates from the Anthropic Interpretability Team.

item.title: Interpretability Dreams
item.description: Our present research aims to create a foundation for mechanistic interpretability research. In doing so, it's important to keep sight of what we're trying to lay the foundations for.

item.title: Softmax Linear Units
item.description: An alternative activation function increases the fraction of neurons which appear to correspond to human-understandable concepts.

item.title: Toy Models of Superposition Elhage et al., 2022
item.description: Neural networks often seem to pack many unrelated concepts into a single neuron - a puzzling phenomenon known as 'polysemanticity'. In our latest interpretability work, we build toy models where the origins and dynamics of polysemanticity can be fully understood.

item.title: Mechanistic Interpretability, Variables, and the Importance of Interpretable Bases
item.description: An informal note on intuitions related to mechanistic interpretability.

item.title: In-Context Learning and Induction Heads Olsson et al., 2022
item.description: An exploration of the hypothesis that induction heads are the primary mechanism behind in-context learning. We also report the existence of a previously unknown phase change in transformers language models.

item.title: Videos
item.description: Very rough informal talks as we search for a way to reverse engineering transformers.

item.title: Garcon
item.description: A description of our tooling for doing interpretability on large models.

item.title: A Mathematical Framework for Transformer Circuits Elhage et al., 2021
item.description: Our early mathematical framework for reverse engineering models, demonstrated by reverse engineering small toy models.

item.title: Exercises
item.description: Some exercises we've developed to improve our understanding of how neural networks implement algorithms at the parameter level.

item.title: Circuits Cross-Post — Activation Oracles
item.description: We train language models to answer questions about their own activations in natural language.

item.title: Automated Auditing
item.description: A note on using agents to perform automated alignment audits, including using interpretability tools.
